equivalence_argument: No complete mapping: a helper exists to filter call-graph contracts by sequencingRights support, but builder and verifier paths use try_classify_static/classify_top_level_with_sr rather than the full call-graph SR-counting path.

checked_queries: classification counts only SR contracts builder verifier
ContractCallInspector sequencing rights full call graph production use
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Builder/verifier classification is not based on the set of call-graph addresses with non-bottom sequencingRights ordering.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-sequencing-rights/src/classifier.rs defines into_classification_with_sr, filtering contract_calls by reader.has_sequencing_rights and returning zero/one/multiple classes.
crates/ethereum/payload/src/sequencing_rights.rs classifies payload candidates with try_classify_static or classify_top_level_with_sr.
crates/ritual-sequencing-rights/src/lib.rs verifier uses try_classify_static or classify_top_level_with_sr; search found no production use of into_classification_with_sr.

notes: The helper is present but unused by the audited builder/verifier paths.
rationale: The commit body states the predicate changed from |CallGraph(tx)| = 1 to counting only call-graph addresses with sequencing rights, and adds into_classification_with_sr for builder/verifier use. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: A transaction's sequencing-rights classification is based on the set of addresses in its call graph for which SequencingRightsReader finds a non-bottom sequencingRights ordering; transactions touching zero such contracts are NoSequencingRights, one such contract are SingleContract, and more than one are MultipleContracts.
suggested_falsification_tests: Simulate a transaction whose call graph touches one contract with sequencingRights and several helper contracts without sequencingRights. Assert classification is SingleContract for the SR contract. Then add a second SR contract in the call graph and assert classification becomes MultipleContracts.
summary: Sequencing classification does not count only SR call-graph contracts in production.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Simulate a transaction touching one SR contract and multiple non-SR helper contracts through internal calls; current production classification will not use the call graph to return SingleContract for the SR contract.
title: Classification counts only SR contracts
